name: IMPACT Community Action
description_short: IMPACT Community Action is a Columbus, OH non-profit serving low-income households up to 200% of the federal poverty level with free community assistance services.
description_long:
IMPACT Community Action is a non-profit community action agency headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, at 711 Southwood Avenue. The organization operates three locations — a main office on the south side and satellite offices at IMPACT North (East 17th Avenue) and IMPACT West (West Broad Street) — enabling it to reach residents across the Columbus metro area. Its mission reflects the community action tradition of empowering individuals and families living in or near poverty to achieve greater economic stability.

The agency serves households based on federal poverty guidelines, with eligibility generally extending up to 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Their website includes a built-in FPL calculator that lets prospective clients instantly check whether their household income qualifies them for assistance based on family size. Services are delivered by appointment, and clients must bring a valid ID and relevant benefit documentation to their visit. Specific program offerings are not detailed on the public website, but community action agencies of this type typically provide assistance with utility bills, housing, food, financial counseling, and case management.

What distinguishes IMPACT is its multi-location model within a single metro area, which reduces transportation barriers for low-income Columbus residents. The organization maintains structured intake protocols — including document checklists and security screening — that indicate a professional, high-volume operation rather than an informal pop-up service. The presence of a multilingual interface and a Federal Poverty Limit calculator reflects a commitment to accessibility for diverse and non-English-speaking populations.

Honestly, the public website provides limited detail about specific programs, eligibility windows, or wait times, which makes it difficult for prospective clients to know exactly what they will receive before arriving. Services are appointment-only and hours are restricted to standard business hours (Monday–Friday, 8:00am–5:00pm), which may be a barrier for working individuals. This is a legitimate, established non-profit and appropriate for residents of Columbus, OH who need free community-based assistance and meet income thresholds up to 200% FPL.
pros: Three Columbus locations (main, north, west) reduce transportation barriers for city residents
Built-in Federal Poverty Level calculator lets visitors instantly check income eligibility before contacting the agency
Serves households up to 200% of FPL — broader eligibility than agencies capped at 100% FPL
Established non-profit with professional intake protocols, document checklists, and security procedures indicating high-volume operations
Multilingual website interface available, improving accessibility for non-English-speaking clients
Free services funded by donations and grants — no fees for clients
Clear contact information and a structured contact form for non-urgent inquiries
cons: Website does not publicly list specific programs or services, requiring clients to call or visit to learn what help is available
Appointment-only model with no walk-in service mentioned — may require wait times not disclosed online
Hours limited to M-F 8:00am–5:00pm, which is a barrier for clients who cannot take time off work
Service area limited to Columbus, OH metro — not available to Ohio residents outside this geography
Strict bag policy (diaper bags, purses, or medical supplies only) may inconvenience clients who need to come directly from work or other obligations
best_for: Columbus, OH residents earning up to 200% of the federal poverty level who need free assistance with essential needs
Low-income households who have time to attend weekday appointments and can gather required documentation in advance
Non-English-speaking individuals in Columbus who need services with multilingual support
People in crisis who need referral to community resources and don't know where to start
diagnosis: IMPACT Community Action is best for low-income Columbus, OH residents who meet the federal poverty income thresholds and need free, in-person assistance from an established community agency. The main caveat is that specific programs and wait times are not disclosed online, so clients should call 614.252.2799 before visiting to confirm what services are currently available and what documentation is required.
services: Federal Poverty Level eligibility screening and calculator tool
In-person case management and intake (by appointment)
Community assistance services for households at 100%–200% FPL
Multi-location service delivery (main office, IMPACT North, IMPACT West)
Multilingual client support (website supports dozens of languages)
Donation-funded free services for qualifying Columbus-area residents
